examples.title: Simple Client-Server Interaction
examples.description: Basic pseudocode demonstrating client-server model in distributed systems
examples.code:
BEGIN
 // Server Process
 PROCEDURE StartServer()
 serverSocket = CreateSocket(PORT)
 WHILE True DO
 clientSocket = serverSocket.Accept()
 REQUEST = clientSocket.Receive()
 RESPONSE = ProcessRequest(REQUEST)
 clientSocket.Send(RESPONSE)
 clientSocket.Close()
 END WHILE
 END PROCEDURE
 
 // Client Process
 PROCEDURE StartClient()
 clientSocket = CreateSocket(SERVER_IP, PORT)
 REQUEST = GetUserRequest()
 clientSocket.Send(REQUEST)
 RESPONSE = clientSocket.Receive()
 DisplayResult(RESPONSE)
 clientSocket.Close()
 END PROCEDURE
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode shows the fundamental client-server interaction: server listens for and processes requests, while client initiates requests and receives responses. This pattern forms the basis of many distributed systems.

examples.title: Middleware Request Routing
examples.description: Demonstrates how middleware provides transparency in distributed systems
examples.code:
BEGIN
 // Middleware Layer
 PROCEDURE HandleClientRequest(request)
 // Location transparency: client doesn't know where service is
 serviceLocation = LookupService(request.serviceName)
 
 // May involve remote communication
 IF serviceLocation.isLocal THEN
 result = ExecuteLocally(request)
 ELSE
 result = ForwardToRemote(serviceLocation, request)
 END IF
 
 // Return result to client - transparent to client
 RETURN result
 END PROCEDURE
 
 // Service Lookup (may use naming service)
 PROCEDURE LookupService(serviceName)
 RETURN NamingService.Resolve(serviceName)
 END PROCEDURE
END
examples.explanation: This demonstrates how middleware provides transparency - clients make requests without knowing whether the service is local or remote, or where exactly it's located. The middleware handles all complexity of location and communication.

examples.title: Distributed Resource Replication
examples.description: Shows replication transparency in distributed systems
examples.code:
BEGIN
 // Replicated Service with Transparency
 VARIABLE replicas = ["Server1", "Server2", "Server3"]
 
 PROCEDURE ReadRequest(resourceId)
 // Replication transparency: user doesn't know about replicas
 // System chooses best replica automatically
 replica = SelectReplica(replicas)
 RETURN replica.Read(resourceId)
 END PROCEDURE
 
 PROCEDURE WriteRequest(resourceId, data)
 // Must update ALL replicas for consistency
 FOR EACH replica IN replicas DO
 replica.Write(resourceId, data)
 END FOR
 RETURN SUCCESS
 END PROCEDURE
 
 PROCEDURE SelectReplica(replicaList)
 // Load balancing: choose least loaded
 RETURN replicaList.FindMin(load)
 END PROCEDURE
END
examples.explanation: This demonstrates replication transparency - users interact with what appears to be a single resource, while the system manages multiple replicas. The code shows both read (load-balanced) and write (synchronized across replicas) operations.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) for write (all replicas), O(1) for read
